vocab.meaning: một môi trường cạnh tranh công bằng
vocab.example: Sports taught me that success is achievable as long as there is a level playing field for every competitor.

vocab.word: get off to a flying start
vocab.meaning: có một khởi đầu vô cùng thuận lợi và thành công
vocab.example: Our football team got off to a flying start by winning the first three matches of the season convincingly.

vocab.meaning: người/đội dẫn đầu (có khả năng thắng cao nhất)
vocab.example: In my school days, our relay team was always considered the front runner in the city championships.

vocab.word: keep one's eye on the ball
vocab.meaning: tập trung cao độ vào mục tiêu quan trọng
vocab.example: The coach always reminded us to keep our eyes on the ball, even when the crowd was incredibly rowdy.

vocab.meaning: đánh bại đối thủ một cách dễ dàng và áp đảo
vocab.example: During the finals, we managed to blow the competition away with our superior tactical coordination.

vocab.meaning: vượt quá khả năng/trình độ của ai đó
vocab.example: I once joined a professional tennis club, but I quickly realized the other players were completely out of my league.

vocab.meaning: ngang tài ngang sức, bám sát nút nhau
vocab.example: The race was neck and neck until the very last second when our team finally pulled ahead.

vocab.meaning: một lựa chọn chắc chắn, ít rủi ro
vocab.example: Choosing him as the captain was a safe bet, given his extensive experience and leadership skills.

vocab.meaning: hành động quá sớm (trước thời điểm đúng/quy định)
vocab.example: I lost the race because I jumped the gun, resulting in a false start that got me disqualified.

vocab.meaning: vượt lên chính mình để xử lý tốt tình huống khó khăn
vocab.example: When our star player was injured, the rest of the team really rose to the occasion to secure the trophy.
